name: Scope Trigger Troubleshooting
description: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide for oscilloscope trigger issues including common problems, setup mistakes, and diagnostic steps.
author: TekAutomate MCP Team

handlerConfig.text: Trigger troubleshooting guide for oscilloscope setup and common issues.

data.summary: Systematic approach to diagnosing and fixing oscilloscope trigger problems.
sequenceHighlights: Verify signal is present and within vertical range
Check trigger source matches the signal channel
Adjust trigger level to intersect signal
Verify trigger type matches signal characteristics
Check horizontal timebase for appropriate frequency display
keyPoints: Signal must be present before trigger will work
Trigger level must be within signal amplitude range
Trigger source must match the active channel
Timebase affects trigger stability for periodic signals
pitfalls: Trigger level set outside signal range
Signal amplitude too small or too large
Incorrect trigger type for signal shape
diagnosticSteps: 1. Verify signal: Set trigger to AUTO, check if waveform appears
2. Check amplitude: Use vertical scale to ensure signal fills screen
3. Verify source: Ensure trigger source matches signal channel
4. Adjust level: Set trigger level to middle of signal amplitude
5. Check coupling: Use DC coupling for most signals, AC for AC-only
6. Adjust timebase: Set to show 2-3 periods of periodic signals
commonFixes: Set TRIGger:A:LEVel to middle of signal amplitude
Change TRIGger:A:SOUrce to correct channel (CH1, CH2, etc.)
Adjust vertical scale (VOLts:DIV) to amplify small signals
Use TRIGger:A:TYPE EDGE for most digital signals
Enable bandwidth limit for noisy signals

name: Measurement Interpretation Guide
description: Guide for interpreting oscilloscope measurements, understanding unexpected values, and troubleshooting measurement accuracy issues.
author: TekAutomate MCP Team

handlerConfig.text: Comprehensive guide for interpreting oscilloscope measurements and troubleshooting accuracy issues.

data.summary: Understanding measurement results, common accuracy issues, and how to validate measurement quality.
sequenceHighlights: Verify measurement type matches what you want to measure
Check acquisition settings affect measurement accuracy
Ensure sufficient signal for reliable measurements
keyPoints: Measurement accuracy depends on signal quality and acquisition settings
Different measurement types require different signal characteristics
Noise and sampling rate can affect measurement results
Some measurements need specific trigger conditions
pitfalls: Insufficient record length for low-frequency measurements
Wrong measurement type for the signal characteristic
commonIssues: Frequency measurements wrong - check timebase and sampling rate
Amplitude measurements vary - enable averaging or high-resolution
Rise time measurements inconsistent - check bandwidth and probe
Duty cycle measurements unstable - use edge trigger and sufficient cycles
solutions: Use ACQuire:MODE AVERAGE for noisy signals
Increase HORizontal:RECOrdlength for better frequency resolution
Set appropriate ACQuire:SAMPling mode for your measurement
Verify probe compensation for accurate amplitude measurements
Use MEASUrement:GATing for specific measurement windows

name: Instrument Connection Problems Guide
description: Systematic troubleshooting for VISA, USB, TCP/IP, and GPIB connection issues with Tektronix instruments.
author: TekAutomate MCP Team

handlerConfig.text: Complete guide for diagnosing and fixing instrument connection problems across different interfaces.

data.summary: Step-by-step troubleshooting for VISA, network, USB, and GPIB connection issues.
sequenceHighlights: Check for error messages and status
keyPoints: Different connection types have different address formats
Network configuration often blocks instrument connections
VISA drivers must match your OS and connection type
Instruments must be powered and responsive for connection
pitfalls: USB drivers not installed or corrupted
GPIB address conflicts or termination issues
troubleshootingSteps: 1. Verify instrument power and front panel response
3. Test with VISA Interactive Test Panel
5. Check Windows Device Manager for hardware issues
6. Test network connectivity with ping command
commonFixes: Update VISA drivers to latest version
Configure firewall exceptions for instrument ports
Set static IP addresses for instruments
Reinstall USB drivers or try different USB port
